top_signals.title: Microsoft Patches a Record 570 Security Flaws
top_signals.summary: Microsoft Corp. today released software updates to plug at least 570 security holes in its Windows operating systems and other software, almost triple the number of vulnerabilities the software giant fixed in its record-smashing Patch Tuesday release last month. Microsoft attributed the burgeoning patch counts to vulnerability discoveries aided by artificial intelligence.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: US Oil, Product Inventories See Builds Across the Board
top_signals.summary: Crude oil inventories in the United States saw an increase of 2.0 million barrels during the week ending July 17, according to new data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) released on Wednesday. The increase brings commercial stockpiles to 411.7 million barrels, according to government data, which are now 6% below the five-year average for this time of year. The EIA’s data release follows API’s figures that were released a day earlier, which reported that crude oil inventories had risen by 2.603 million barrels in…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Bad Loans Threaten Argentina’s Next Engine of Economic Growth
top_signals.summary: Bad loans keep piling up in Argentina, dashing hopes that the economic pain unleashed by President Javier Milei’s harsh austerity program had already crested.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: ‘Optimistic signals’ meet a $68,000 wall as bitcoin’s recovery faces its next test: analysts
top_signals.summary: Bitcoin holds above $65,800 as spot ETFs log a sixth day of inflows. Bitfinex analysts flag $68K resistance as the next test.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Pakistan launches crypto investigation unit to crack down on digital asset crimes: report
top_signals.summary: FIA's Muhammad Athar Waheed suggested that other major agencies form dedicated teams to tackle digital asset crimes.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.
